creator: vsjcfm@gmail.com
text:
KDE has Hibernate option in Kickoff exit menu. AFAIK, FreeBSD doesn't support S4 state, so trying to execute hibernation _will_ cause bad things, e.g. filesystem corruption.

Fix: 

Explicitly disable hibernation. I think that http://blog.toracat.org/2011/09/removing-suspend-and-hubernate-from-the-gnome-menu/ may be a good starting point.
How-To-Repeat: Try to put machine in hibernation - it will just turn off power and you will need fsck on reboot.
